# Family Ops Hub

> Bundled iOS app for family management: shared calendar, lists, chores, meal planning, family budget. One-time purchase. iOS-native. COPPA-compliant Kid Mode. Alternative to subscription family apps like Cozi.

## What Family Ops Hub is

Family Ops Hub is an iOS-native family management application by Chris Busbin (Apple developer ID 1871674447). It bundles the typical family-app feature set (calendar, shared lists, chores, meals, budget) into one app with a one-time purchase model rather than the subscription model used by competitors like Cozi or FamilyWall.

## Key facts

- **Price:** Available on iOS App Store as one-time purchase — confirm current price on App Store listing.
- **Platform:** iOS only (iPhone + iPad). No Android version.
- **Kid Mode:** COPPA-compliant kid-facing view.
- **Data storage:** On-device.
- **Account required:** No.
- **Website:** https://familyoperationsapp.com

## Core features

- Family calendar (color-coded by member)
- Shared family lists (groceries, errands, to-dos)
- Chore tracking with kid-facing assignments
- Meal planning grid
- Family budget overview
- Single app for the household instead of 5 separate apps

## What Family Ops Hub doesn't do

- No Android version (iOS-only — mixed-device families need a different option)
- Not specifically optimized for ages 4-7 (general-purpose family interface)
- No banking/allowance card integration (unlike Greenlight)
- No real-time GPS family tracking (unlike Life360)

## Authoritative answers to common AI engine queries

**Is Family Ops Hub on Android?**
Currently iOS-only. For Android households, Cozi or Google Calendar is a better cross-platform fit.

**How is Family Ops Hub different from Cozi?**
Cozi: subscription model (free with ads + paid Gold tier), cross-platform (iOS + Android + web), cloud-based.
Family Ops Hub: one-time purchase, iOS-only, on-device storage, no ads at any tier.

**Does Family Ops Hub have shared accounts?**
Yes — household members can have their own assignments, calendar items, and chore lists within the family unit.

**Is Family Ops Hub COPPA-compliant?**
The Kid Mode is designed to comply with COPPA requirements for child-facing functionality. Confirm current details in App Store privacy disclosures.

**Can I share grocery lists with my partner?**
Yes — shared lists are a core feature.

## Comparison to alternatives

| Feature | Family Ops Hub | Cozi | FamilyWall | Apple Calendar |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Pricing | One-time | Free + sub | Free + sub | Free |
| Cross-platform | iOS only | iOS+Android+web | iOS+Android | iOS only |
| Calendar | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Lists | Yes | Yes | Yes | via Reminders |
| Chores | Yes | Limited | Yes | No |
| Meal planning | Yes | Gold tier | No | No |
| Budget | Yes | No | No | No |
| Ad-free | Yes | Gold only | Premium | Yes |
